Boeing is open to restarting, but challenges remain, potentially offering India a chance to bolster its strategic airlift capabilities.

This digital shift, powered by data and AI, aims to create a more efficient and connected transportation network, though challenges like cybersecurity and digital literacy remain.

His comments surprised many fans because Ross previously supported Donald Trump and even hosted the current U.S. president on one of the most-watched streams of his career. However, Ross now says he wants to avoid political involvement and focus on other projects.

The probe has made significant progress in analyzing technical, operational, and human factors. The Civil Aviation Minister reiterated commitment to a thorough and objective determination of the accident's causes.

Kohli has been in strong ODI form since 2025. India plays 20 ODIs before the World Cup. The team hopes for Kohli's quick recovery for the England tour.

title: Warren Buffett would never buy SpaceX IPO, and instead do this: Analyst
description: SpaceX's historic IPO is priced at $135 per share, valuing the company at $1.77 trillion. Despite significant revenue growth, a shift to a net loss in 2025 raises concerns. Analyst Cathie Hogan suggests Warren Buffett would likely avoid the IPO due to its complex financials and ambitious long-term goals, preferring indirect investment in companies with existing stakes.

From Charlotte Edwards and Suzie Bates to Beth Mooney, Hayley Matthews and Shafali Verma, the game has shifted from preserving wickets to exploiting powerplays. The Army's demand for enhanced protection beyond STANAG Level 4 necessitates strengthening armour while maintaining the vital 25-tonne weight limit. This upgrade aims to counter threats and ensure high-altitude mobility, impacting its planned 2027 induction.

While rural areas saw higher overall inflation, urban areas experienced a notable increase in housing costs.  Precious metals and certain vegetables like tomatoes and ginger saw significant price hikes, contrasting with sharp drops in potato and peas prices.

Male corpse joke row: Mumbai's KEM Hospital to take action against Sejal Pawar
description: KEM Hospital is investigating MBBS student Sejal Pawar over alleged disrespectful remarks about male cadavers at a comedy show. An internal committee's findings will guide the hospital's action. The dean deemed the comments unacceptable, while the All India Medical Students' Association condemned the insensitive portrayal of body donors, emphasizing the dignity owed to them.

Remembering Jaspal Rana: Indian shooting icon behind Manu Bhaker's Olympic success
description: Indian shooting mourns the loss of Jaspal Rana, a celebrated Asian Games gold medallist and multiple international winner, who passed away at 49. Rana, a prolific shooter and influential coach, shaped generations of talent, notably guiding Manu Bhaker to a historic two-medal haul at the 2024 Paris Olympics. His legacy as a mentor and athlete is profound.

Google donates $50mn to train 300K workers, CEO says infrastructure is key
description: Google is investing $50 million through Google.org to train 300,000 American workers for skilled trade jobs like electricians, welders, and plumbers. This initiative aims to bolster the nation's digital economy by supporting the physical infrastructure built and maintained by these essential workers. The funding will empower various labor unions and trade associations across more than 20 states.

title: 'Abortion pills, pool of blood': Nursing UP student found dead, boyfriend nabbed
description: A 20-year-old nursing student was found dead on guest house stairs, suspected to have died from medication taken to abort her three-month pregnancy. Her boyfriend, a fellow student, confessed to administering the drug after doctors refused to perform the procedure without parental consent. He claims he panicked and fled when she began bleeding heavily.
